channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Here's what two undecided Wisconsin voters are holding out for in the 2024 election
item.description: A small percentage of voters in battleground state Wisconsin are still undecided, but that sliver of voters could decide the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on affordable housing disappearing across the U.S.
item.description: While Americans continue to struggle under high rents, as many as 223,0000 affordable housing units could disappear in the next five years alone.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Plot to Kill Trump: Back to Butler
item.description: Former President Donald Trump returned to Butler, Pennsylvania, three months after surviving an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: A visual look at Trump's return to Pennsylvania for a rally at site of assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Thousands of supporters returned to Butler, Pennsylvania, on Saturday to rally around Donald Trump at the same site where a gunman tried to assassinate him in July. Many had lined up as the sun rose for the event billed as a “tribute to the American spirit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris pledges ongoing federal support as she visits North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris pledged ongoing federal support and praised the “heroes among us” as she visited North Carolina on Saturday in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, her second trip in four days to the disaster zone.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is returning to Butler, the site of the 1st assassination attempt against him
item.description: The rally will mark Trump's first time back in the Pennsylvania city since the failed assassination attempt against him in July. The guest list will include a shooting survivor, as well as Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Trump urges his supporters to deliver victory in his return to scene of first assassination attempt
item.description: Donald Trump rallied supporters at the same Pennsylvania grounds where a gunman tried to assassinate him in July, setting aside near-constant worries for his physical safety.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC Councilman Joe Borelli on whether Mayor Eric Adams should resign
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams is facing mounting pressure following his indictment, with more federal corruption charges possible. Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Adams should resign, according to a Marist College poll released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: County official accuses two supervisors of preventing his promotion in filed claim
item.description: A former Registrar of Voters filed a claim against the county of San Diego, specifically making allegations against two sitting members on the Board of Supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed Capitol is sentenced to probation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed the U.S. Capitol with a mob of Donald Trump supporters was sentenced to probation instead of prison on Friday, as the federal courts reached a milestone in the punishment of Capitol rioters.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden talks election, economy and Middle East in surprise news briefing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After 1,080 days as president, Joe Biden on Friday decided to pop in and take questions in the White House briefing room for the first time, striding in with a grin after a strong monthly jobs report and the temporary settlement of a strike by ports workers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China could wage economic war on Taiwan to force surrender, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. military officials and analysts have for years warned of possible armed attacks or blockades by China on Taiwan, but a report released Friday has raised a red flag about possible non-military tactics that could be used effectively against the self-governed island.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former NIH official accused of making emails 'disappear' pleads Fifth to COVID subcommittee
item.description: A National Institutes of Health employee who served the agency for over three decades is pleading the Fifth after the House COVID Subcommittee demanded her testimony on allegedly making documents "disappear" for coworkers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: The US and South Korea reach new deal on costs for the American troop presence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States and South Korea have tentatively agreed to a new deal covering the costs of maintaining the American military presence there, the U.S. State Department and South Korean foreign ministry announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's campaign says he raised $160 million in September, ended month with $283 million banked
item.description: Former President Donald Trump raised $160 million for his campaign in September and entered October with $283 million in the bank for the campaign's final sprint, his aides announced.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Politically charged rumors and conspiracy theories about Helene flourish on X
item.description: False claims about the hurricane and the government’s response got millions of views on social media. Emergency management researchers say it makes it harder for useful information to surface.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Republicans lead September voter registration charge in Nevada
item.description: Nonpartisan voters were just ahead of Republicans registering to vote in Nevada during September. Democrats lagged in third.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: 22nd Congressional District Debate with David Valadao and Rudy Salas
item.description: The two candidates seeking election to California's 22nd Congressional District will face off tonight.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: DHS warns of risks around Oct. 7, November election in annual threat assessment
item.description: “We are, of course, aware that that anniversary may add even more fuel to an already challenging and heightened threat environment,” a DHS official said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Walz’s account of son’s brush with gun violence offers poignant moment in VP debate
item.description: “Look, I’ve got a 17-year-old and he witnessed a shooting at a community center playing volleyball,” Walz said during the debate, referencing his son Gus. “Those things don’t leave you.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Should moderators fact-check? Veteran broadcast debate host, journalism professor weigh in
item.description: MPR News host emeritus Gary Eichten and University of Minnesota associate professor and director of the Minnesota Journalism Center Benjamin Toff joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to talk about the positives and perils of live political debates.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: As Jimmy Carter celebrates his 100th birthday, a look through the decades
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter is turning 100. Here are images from notable events or parts of his life, both during his presidency and after.
item.dc:creator: James Doubek, MPR News

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News
